title: erodium chium subsp. littoreum
intro: Erodium chium subsp. littoreum, also known as the sea storksbill, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the geranium family, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and hairy stems. The medicinal properties of Erodium chium subsp. littoreum are attributed to its flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Erodium chium subsp. littoreum can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies in the form of infusions, decoctions, and salves.
